label: p_stage3_bundle_chain_match
note: Bundle-Laplacian chain matcher (Stage 3 G4 + G5, framing-corrected per hostile review). Source spectra from bin/p7_3_bundle_laplacian_omega_fix. PRIMARY mapping is unit_scaled (λ = k_X · λ_min) — the only dimensionally consistent comparison between dimensionful Bochner-Laplacian eigenvalues and dimensionless chain positions. Direct (λ = k_X) and MassSquared (λ = 2^k_X) are diagnostic baselines only. Each (candidate × chain) row is split into testable/untestable counts at the supplied basis: positions with predicted_lambda > lambda_max·1.05 cannot be tested at this basis truncation and are excluded from the headline match count. The k=13 D_8 e_4 entry is a DM-candidate diagnostic slot, not a framework prediction; it is OUT-OF-BASIS at the current Schoen/AKLP basis (lambda_max ≈ 13) and any near-miss is a basis-edge artifact.

summary.headline: 1 of 6 testable positions match at 5% strict (UnitScaled) — consistent with uniform-null coincidence
summary.interpretation: At basis with lambda_max=12.997, 6 of 12 framework chain positions exceed the spectral range and are structurally untestable. The 1 testable match(es) under UnitScaled is/are consistent with random clustering at p=0.99. Decisive test requires higher basis (BBW-predicted dim ~35-40) and G1 mode-degeneracy fix. Direct mapping is dimensionally inconsistent (eigenvalues have units 1/length², k_X is dimensionless) and reported only as diagnostic. k=13 D_8 e_4 slot is OUT-OF-BASIS at this truncation — any near-miss is a basis-edge artifact, not a DM-candidate match.
